channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Live updates: Trump defends tariffs; Billionaire Trump endorser calls for trade war time-out
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are on defense amid the fallout from President Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy. Markets took a nosedive Thursday following Trump’s announcement of a general 10 percent tariff on goods imported to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Forecast of a week of rain adds to woes faced by victims of Myanmar quake, as death toll tops 3,500
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — People in Myanmar’s earthquake-stricken areas braced for thunderstorms late Sunday, after heavy rains and winds the previous night disrupted rescue and relief operations and added to the misery of the many who lost their homes in the disaster and were forced to sleep in the open.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine will send a team to the US next week for talks on a new draft mineral deal
item.description: Ukraine's economy minister has told The Associated Press it will send a delegation to Washington next week for talks on a new draft of a proposed mineral deal with the U.S. The negotiations mark a renewed push to finalize an agreement that could establish a joint investment fund.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Stellacci, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 1 killed in Russian attack on Kyiv as death toll from earlier missile strike rises to 20
item.description: Kyiv's Mayor Vitali Klitschko says one person was was killed as Russian air strikes hit the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen’s Houthi rebels killed at least six people.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: In its 3-year-old war, Russia has detained what human rights activists say are thousands of Ukrainian civilians and has charged them with various crimes.
item.dc:creator: Dasha Litvinova And Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canadian police say arrest made after man barricaded himself inside Parliament's east block
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Police said a man was arrested late Saturday after an hours-long lockdown on Parliament Hill in Canada's capital. Investigators said the man gained unauthorized access to the east block of Canada's parliament on Saturday afternoon and barricaded himself inside the building.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli troops deploy to a new security corridor across southern Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli troops deployed to a newly established security corridor across southern Gaza, the military announced Saturday, as pressure on the Hamas militant group increased weeks into the renewed war.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea presidential ouster part of Chinese strategy to 'expand its regional influence,' expert says
item.description: One analyst, as well as supporters of South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, says China backed Yoon's ouster in South Korea to weaken U.S. ties and expand its influence by propping up Beijing-friendly political allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China slaps a 34% tax on all US imports in retaliation for Trump's tariffs
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tax on all U.S. imports next week, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new tariffs that delivered the strongest response yet from Beijing to the American leader's trade war.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump's latest tariffs could affect your wallet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump has unveiled his latest tariffs, and they could have significant implications for your wallet. Trump's sweeping new tariffs, on top of previous levies and retaliation worldwide, are expected to increase prices for everyday items.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and CORA LEWIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's parliament passes bill that would change Muslim land endowments
item.description: India's parliament passes a bill amending Muslim land endowments, sparking protests over concerns it undermines minority rights and could lead to property confiscation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 US preorders over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nintendo said U.S. preorders of its Switch 2 console will not take place on April 9 as it assesses tariff implications.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: British police charge comedian Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sri Lanka's target to be free of land mines by 2028 is under threat as US reviews aid
item.description: MANKULAM, Sri Lanka (AP) — Thavarathnam Pushparani fought on the front lines for the now-defeated Tamil Tiger rebels against the Sri Lankan forces in its decadeslong separatist war and later took to clearing the land mines on the same battle lines.
item.dc:creator: ERANGA JAYAWARDENA and KRISHAN FRANCIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yoon Suk Yeol had a rapid rise in South Korean politics before an abrupt downfall
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Yoon Suk Yeol’s political rise was fast: the former star prosecutor clinched South Korea’s presidency only a year after he entered politics.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to expect in South Korea now that Yoon has been removed from office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The South Korean Constitutional Court’s decision to formally unseat President Yoon Suk Yeol is another test for the country’s democracy after the conservative-liberal divide deepened over his imposition of martial law and subsequent impeachment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Tunisia, snails inch toward replacing red meat as people turn to cheaper protein
item.description: AKOUDA, Tunisia (AP) — In fields outside their hometown in central Tunisia, an increasing number of unemployed young men are seeking a new way to make a living, picking snails off of rocks and leaves and collecting them in large plastic bags to take to the local market to be sold.
item.dc:creator: MEHDI EL AREM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio demands NATO pony up, dismisses 'hysteria' over US role under Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NATO allies they must make hard choices and increase defense spending to 5% to bolster capabilities in face of emboldened adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli defense minister says military is expanding its operation in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s military operation in the Gaza Strip is expanding to seize “large areas,” the defense minister said Wednesday, as airstrikes on the southern city of Khan Younis killed 17 people overnight, according to hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants new Iran nuclear deal after he withdrew from the first
item.description: Trump withdrew the U.S from the deal during his first term, claiming it was “the worst and most one-sided transaction.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel prepares to move deeper into Gaza and orders Rafah evacuated
item.description: The Israeli military indicated on Monday it could soon launch another major offensive in Rafah and ordered most of the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip evacuated. Israel ended its ceasefire with Hamas and renewed its air and ground war earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
